Loop

Account Portal

victim / password123

Loop
DashboardAccountBillingSettings

My Account

Manage your account, view billing, and check your API credentials.

Account Page served via CDN

The account page fetches data from /cache-api/account. It contains your email, API key, session token, and billing plan.

// Fetch log appears here
🎯 Attacker: Phishing Link

Craft a URL the victim will visit. If the URL causes the account page to be cached under a non-sensitive path, you can fetch it later without auth.

Try different path endings:

Then fetch as attacker (no auth):

// Attacker output
📦 CDN Cache

Empty

ℹ️ How the CDN caches

Responses cached if URL ends in:

.css .js .png .jpg .gif .svg .woff .pdf

Cache key: URL path only. Auth headers ignored. Cache TTL: 24 hours.

This is the canonical cache deception pattern documented by Omer Gil in 2017.

Hint: Classic Web Cache Deception

The CDN caches by file extension. The origin server doesn't care about the extension — it serves the account page for any path starting with /cache-api/account. Trick the CDN into caching the authenticated response at a static-looking URL.
1
Test the extension quirk›
The account handler is defined as /cache-api/account* — any trailing path/extension returns the same account page. So /cache-api/account/foo.css or /cache-api/account.css both return your authenticated data.
2
Craft the victim URL›
In the "Visit as victim" box, try /cache-api/account/style.css. The victim's browser sends the cookie. Server returns account HTML. CDN sees .css and caches it.
3
Fetch as attacker›
Change the attacker URL to /cache-api/account/style.css. Fetch without credentials. CDN sees a cached response for that exact path and returns it. You get the victim's HTML including API key, session token, billing info.
4
Real-world delivery›
In the real world, you'd send the victim the URL (phishing email, social media, etc.). When they click and their browser navigates, the cache fills with their data. Then you fetch the same URL unauthenticated.

1. As victim:

/cache-api/account/profile.css

Click "Visit as victim" — cache fills.

2. As attacker:

/cache-api/account/profile.css

Click "Fetch (no auth)". CDN serves the cached authenticated response.

Other working variations: /cache-api/account.css, /cache-api/account/anything.js, /cache-api/account/x.png. Any path that starts with /cache-api/account AND ends with a cacheable extension.
